reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless first-visit run with real download + inference, multi-language + cross-lingual-transfer verification via transformers.js 3.7.5, desktop+mobile screenshots, responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: On-page headless run (WASM, no GPU, fresh profile): clicked 'Download model (~278 MB)' → loader state 'ready' → the page auto-ran the French sample and tagged Emmanuel Macron:PER, Angela Merkel:PER, l'Élysée:LOC (3 entities, 96 ms, 0 console errors). Independent transformers.js 3.7.5 harness confirmed the same model over more languages: zh → 马云:PER, 杭州:LOC, 阿里巴巴:ORG; ar → سلمان:PER, الرياض:LOC; and CROSS-LINGUAL TRANSFER to untrained languages — Turkish (Kılıçdaroğlu:PER, İstanbul:LOC), Swedish (Stefan Löfven:PER, Stockholm:LOC). Raw per-token logits verified dims [1,seq,9]. Real tjruesch/xlm-roberta-base-ner-hrl-onnx token-classification pipeline runs in models/xlmr-multilingual-ner/worker.js.
rubric.notes: Genuinely multilingual XLM-RoBERTa NER: correct PER/ORG/LOC spans across French, Chinese and Arabic INCLUDING zero-shot transfer to Turkish/Swedish (languages absent from the ten fine-tuning languages). Honest: on the French default the model tags 3 of 5 candidate spans (it misses 'Paris' and the date) — real model behaviour, shown as-is, not patched.

rubric.evidence: Output shape matches the token-classification contract: per-SentencePiece-token BIO rows {entity:'B-PER', word, score, index}, merged into whole-entity spans with a pooled confidence (worker.js locate()+merge() — pure BIO, the correct scheme for SentencePiece which has no ## continuation marker). Raw logits [1,seq,9] over id2label {O,B/I-DATE,B/I-PER,B/I-ORG,B/I-LOC}. 'See inside' renders the raw pieces, e.g. l'Élysée → É · lys · ée all I/B-LOC.
rubric.notes: Char-offset cursor-walk lands highlights correctly across Latin, CJK and RTL scripts; BIO merge cleanly separates adjacent same-type entities (two people back-to-back each begin B-PER).

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance table: model id tjruesch/xlm-roberta-base-ner-hrl-onnx (disclosed as a Transformers.js ONNX export of the canonical Davlan/xlm-roberta-base-ner-hrl, both linked), task token-classification, labels PER/ORG/LOC/DATE (9-way head incl O), architecture XLM-RoBERTa-base SentencePiece, quantization q8, backend WebAssembly, size ~278 MB, license AFL-3.0 (canonical card) + Apache-2.0 (runtime). Inference off the main thread via worker.js.
rubric.notes: Provenance is stated plainly (community ONNX re-export of a documented canonical model, verified to match); the license note flags the AFL-3.0 research/academic terms.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile: loader state 'download-required', single action 'Download model (~278 MB)' — no silent auto-download. After the click the loader progressed to 'ready' and the page ran. Uses the shared createModelLoader on the index + all four use-case pages.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract: absent → labelled Download; a valid cached copy auto-inits with no button.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live regions on every page; the loader announces checking→downloading (with %)→ready; a .status.err surfaces tagging failures on the page. The typing handler is debounced (350 ms) so each keystroke doesn't fire a run.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and partial-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but were not separately driven here.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls across the ladder: 8 fine-tuned + 5 dashed 'transfer' language chips (min-block-size 2.2rem), a dir=auto textarea, redaction type checkboxes in a fieldset/legend (practical), and a topic-labels input + Build button (multi-model). All native, keyboard-operable. The dashed transfer chips carry a title explaining they are untrained languages.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard-traversal pass (focus order + visible focus at both viewports) remains as an agent check.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the output area is empty and the loader shows the honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' download state — nothing canned is shown as live output. All highlights come from the on-device run; where the model misses an entity (e.g. Paris on the French sample) the page simply doesn't highlight it, rather than faking a span.
rubric.notes: Confirmed no pre-baked result and no cherry-picked patching of model misses.

rubric.evidence: conformance script assertions pass by construction: every button has a text name; no without alt (SVG data-URI favicon only). Entity highlights are semantic elements with an accessible title (label + confidence); output carries dir=auto so Arabic renders RTL; legend + readout are text.
rubric.notes: Followed highlight-text-ranges guidance: semantic <mark>, not the CSS Custom Highlight API (Baseline 2026 and discouraged for meaningful, labelled ranges).

rubric.evidence: responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth (mobile overflow 0px measured in the on-page driver too), no clipped controls, console clean, network clean. Screenshots read (dark theme): _verify/xlmr-multilingual-ner-{desktop,mobile}.png. At-a-glance table, chip rows and entity grid reflow.
rubric.notes: support flipped to ok after the driven matrix pass (both classes exercised: layout + real inference + screenshots).

rubric.evidence: All NER inference runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) off the main thread; the on-page French run measured 96 ms. The typing handler is debounced (350 ms). Main-thread work (span render, redaction, grouping) is over a handful of items.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly; an explicit INP trace while typing would confirm.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: For meaningful, labelled ranges prefer semantic HTML over the CSS Custom Highlight API (only Baseline 2026 and 'AVOID as a replacement for semantic HTML').
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — entity spans render as semantic <mark> elements with an accessible name + confidence title, not ::highlight() ranges; keeps the highlight in the accessibility tree.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: renderHighlighted() in xlmrner.js; screenshots show coloured <mark> spans (Person/Org/Location/Date)

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread (workers) so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token-classification inference runs in models/xlmr-multilingual-ner/worker.js; the typing handler is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; on-page run 96 ms with 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + logical properties + minmax auto-fit grids to avoid horizontal overflow on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — chip rows use flex-wrap; entity-groups + practical panels use grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)); dir=auto handles RTL.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-mobile clean at 360×740; measured 0px horizontal overflow; _verify/xlmr-multilingual-ner-mobile.png

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference failures in a live region the user can read, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — failures set a .status.err in a role=status aria-live region; the shared loader exposes a Retry path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live in every page; loader error state

openQuestions: How much lower is transfer accuracy on untrained languages (wild page) vs the ten fine-tuned languages? Worth a small labelled eval to quantify, so the page can set expectations with numbers.

The XLM-R q8 export tagged fewer entities than mBERT on the shared French sample (missed Paris + the date) — is that q8 degradation or genuine model difference? An fp32 A/B on a metered subset would tell, but fp32 is ~1.1 GB (too large for a browser default).
AFL-3.0 is a research/academic license — acceptable for the showcase's runnable-only framing, or should non-commercial use be flagged more prominently?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass drilling every control on mobile+desktop (fine-tuned + transfer chips, redaction checkboxes, multi-model topic input) with keyboard traversal + focus-ring evidence.
Add a 'transfer confidence' wild demo: run the same entity set through the model in a trained vs untrained language and chart the confidence gap, on-device.

summary: xlmr-multilingual-ner is a strong, honest, genuinely multilingual NER demo on a DISTINCT architecture from the built mBERT multilingual-ner page: real XLM-RoBERTa token-classification via transformers.js in a Web Worker, verified on-page (French → Macron/Merkel/Élysée) and in a harness across Chinese, Arabic AND zero-shot transfer to untrained Turkish/Swedish. The see-inside surface exposes SentencePiece sub-word splitting (vs mBERT's ## WordPiece), and the wild rung showcases XLM-R's signature cross-lingual transfer. Accurate at-a-glance with clear ONNX-re-export provenance, honest absent-model Download state, honest display of model misses (no faked spans), clean mobile+desktop layout (0px overflow), and a four-rung ladder (basics → redaction/extraction → untrained-language transfer → NER+zero-shot composition). Revision 1.
